MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard in Department 608 of the San Francisco Superior Court before Judge Robert Foley. Department 204 referred all interested parties to Department 608. This matter was passed until 9:30 a.m. and then called in Department 608 to accommodate the arrival of any interested parties.

Appearances: Robert Cowan for and with Alvin Hines (Administrator). Karen Taylor (Heir) is present in pro per. Ping Huang Westbrook (Buyer) is present. Gloria Chun (Agent) is also present.

Mr. Cowan stated that there was a buyer that had backed out and that a second petition was then filed on December 26, 2019. The Court briefly addressed notice and stated that the buyer needed to be served. Ping Huang Westbrook is present and voir dired by the Court. Ping Huang Westbrook waived her right to written notice. Mr. Cowan addressed notice stating that Karen Taylor, one of the heirs, only provided an email address and refused to give her physical address. The Court briefly addressed the notice requirements. Mr. Cowan stated that Karen Taylor was present in court today. Karen Taylor was voir dired by the Court and waived written notice. Mr. Cowan confirmed for the Court that the Inventory and Appraisal had been filed and stated that the sale will close in thirty days.

There being no overbids, the sale was confirmed at $1,395,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property at 1800 Palou Avenue, San Francisco, CA (subject to review of a revised proposed order by Mr. Cowan). The net sale proceeds are to be placed in a blocked account. The Court stated that it finds that it is in the best interest to confirm the sale with the following note: that there was an initial offer that fell through which resulted in Mr. Cowan filing a second petition (Westbrook). The Court waived any irregularity as to the scrivener's error with regard to the amount of the first overbid in the report of sale. The Court further noted that box 10 in the report of sale stated the correct amount of the first overbid and that box 11 contained the scrivener's error. The Court also waived any irregularity as to the overbid amount since no overbidders appeared in court today.

A status hearing on the filing of a receipt for the funds placed in a blocked account is set for: February 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. The Court stated that if there is proof on file and the proof is sufficient that the matter may be taken off calendar.

Judge: Robert Foley. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. No bailiff. Matter not reported. (D608 for D204)

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)
